text: Sodium supplement intake has no statistically significant impact on pace, race time, or finishing position in ultramarathon runners.
Dehydrated runners outperform overhydrated runners in ultramarathons, with 3.6 min/km faster pace and 4.6 hours faster finishing times.
specific_value: 3.6 min/km faster pace, 4.6 h faster finish

text: Among top 25th percentile ultramarathon finishers, dehydration is nearly four times more common than overhydration (11% vs 2.8%).
specific_value: 11% dehydrated vs 2.8% overhydrated in top 25th percentile

text: Performance is poorly correlated with hydration status when controlled for weight-adjusted sodium intake during ultramarathons.
Overhydration should be avoided during ultramarathon racing, as it is associated with worse performance outcomes compared to mild dehydration.
